text: The NFL doesn’t need to as much though because they are a behemoth and people love the game of football so much, they’ll watch even if their team isn’t playing. They benefit from scarcity. They set viewership records year after year basically regardless of who is good and who isn’t.           The NFL will break viewership records for the next superbowl, even if Green Bay and Buffalo are the matchup. Market size doesn’t really affect them, nor do they live and die by super stars.           Additionally, the NFL meta constantly changes. The game is not “solved” like basketball is where there is one clear winning play style (3s, FTs, layups). And if it were, the NFL isn’t afraid to change the rules (kickoffs, extra points).        It’s very clear that the other big sports leagues still care about the sport itself, while Adam Silver views the actual sport of basketball as secondary to being an “entertainment product” to be consumed.           I think this will genuinely hurt the NBA in the long run, even if short term gains might be good. I believe they’re basically mortgaging the future to try to build interest/fan engagement now.
label: analysis

text: The greater threat was the 3 pointer from Brunson, with the amount of time left, you wouldn't expect there to be time for a rebound and second shot. The default move is to wind clock down and then take on last shot at it to deny them a chance to score. I was surprised at the depth and range of that shot.    Brunson taking the shot so early in the time clock and from so deep was a *to me* unexpected move. It had possibly intentional effect of pulling Wemby out to give OG a chance at the rebound/tip-in.    The luck factor was that a shot 31 feet away doesn't typically have such a soft bounce. The gifted athlete part was timing that jump and the legendary part was keeping such a soft touch when his adrenaline was at 1000x. Ask Josh Hart about it. Or Wemby.    I want Josh Hart to have a chance to get the go ahead score next game. He doesn't need it, it won't change narrative of how awesome he is or how much we love him, just got sense in that interview that he feels he let himself down. And to that I say, Mr. Hart, give yourself some credit, your rebounds won game 1, you had plenty of other clutch moments in this game, and bro, it was closing seconds of a NBA finals historic comeback.wemby choked 2 free throws and threw ball away to lose game 2.
